ogTitle: Lesmurdie vs Lime Lake: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lesmurdie and Lime Lake.
heading: Lesmurdie vs Lime Lake — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lesmurdie, Western Australia and Lime Lake, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lesmurdie has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Lime Lake (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lesmurdie or Lime Lake safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lesmurdie has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Lime Lake scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lesmurdie scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Lime Lake scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lesmurdie or Lime Lake?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lesmurdie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
